The recurring "relay rejected event: write actions are disabled" errors occur as the daemon revives ~10 old sessions and tries publishing domain events to wss://nip29.f7z.io; the relay rejects all writes but the daemon continues spawning sessions anyway. The likely cause is either a temporary relay read-only state or a race condition during NIP-42 auth, not a code bug.
why does the relay say "write actions are disabled." -- look at its logs (ssh into pablo@157.180.102.242)

The relay never actually sends that message — it originates client-side in the nostr-relay-pool Rust crate when the daemon's local RelayServiceFlags for nip29.f7z.io is missing the WRITE flag, so the event never reaches the wire. The intermittent failures during startup suggest a relay-pool state race as ~10 reconciled sessions publish simultaneously through one shared Transport.
